title: Living in Wildwood, MO — Complete 2026 Guide
content:
Wildwood represents the largest municipality in St. Louis County, spanning over 66 square miles with a population exceeding 35,000 residents. In 2024, Niche.com ranked it as the #1 best St. Louis suburb to raise a family.

Location & Geography: Situated in West County with rolling hills, lush greenery, and plenty of parks. Expect a 30-45 minute commute to downtown St. Louis via I-64 and I-44.

Housing Options: Large subdivisions with amenities (pools, tennis courts), attached and detached villas, condos, rural properties with acreage, custom estates and equestrian properties. Notable neighborhoods: Villages at Brightleaf, Westglen Farms, Main Street Crossing, Cherry Hills, Westhampton Woods, Wildhorse Parkway.

Schools: All served by Rockwood School District (53,000+ households). Top high schools: Lafayette, Marquette, Eureka.

Parks & Recreation: Babler State Park, Rockwoods Reservation, Bluffview Park, Greensfelder Park, Al Foster Trail, Wildwood Community Park. Wildwood Town Center offers shopping, dining, fitness, Starbucks, Dierbergs, and a hotel. Weekend farmers markets Saturdays 8am-noon.

New Developments: The Reserve by McKelvey Homes (115 homes), Internet Access Project ($6.75M bringing Spectrum to rural areas), Crestview and Latitude N38 in Town Center.

Advantages: Abundant outdoor spaces, diverse housing for varying budgets, strong community amenities, historic sites dating to 1800s including Route 66, excellent schools. Considerations: Extended commute, narrow hilly roads in western areas, limited high-speed internet in some neighborhoods, some properties use septic/well water.
